title: chamaesyce katrajensis var. kasaragodensis
intro: Chamaesyce katrajensis var. kasaragodensis, also known as the Kasaragod Spurge or a variety of the Katraj Spurge, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chamaesyce katrajensis var. kasaragodensis include relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as protection against microbial infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
